content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CoprMrssroN
428 J Street o Sr.rire 620 o Sacramento, CA gigll_23zg
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
May 4,2O10
Matthew L. Goetz
P. O. Box 6924
Moraga, CA94570
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A.-10-056
Dear Mr. Goetz:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest code
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act,,).1
Please note that this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair political practices
Commission ("the Commission") does not act as a findir of fact when it renders assistance. (In
re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.)
QUESTIONS
1. Are you required to file a Statement of Economic Interests ("Form 7OO-) when your
position is not listed in your agency's conflict-of-interest code?
2. Does Regulation 18734 apply to your position, which is an existing classification in
your agency?
CONCLUSION
l. If your position has decision-making authority, your agency is required to .,designate,,
it in the agency's conflict of interest code, requiring you to file uitot"rn"nt oi ".oro1nic 
interests.
However, until your position is added to your agency's conflict of interest code, there is no
requirement for you to file the Form 700. However, please note that all public officials
(regardless of whether they file Form 700s) are subject to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections 8 1000 throug h glol4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the piir political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through lll997 of Title 2 of thi Calittrrnia code of Regulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
-il&


File No. A-10-056
Page No. 2
2. No. If your position is an existing classification within your agency, it is not
considered a "new position" and thus would not be covered under ft.egula-tion 18734.
FACTS
You are currently employed by the Califomia Department of Transportation (,.Caltrans,')
in the classification of Senior Transportation Surveyor 1'tTS"; in Regionai Area, District 4, (San
Francisco Bay Area).
The STS classification has been in use by Caltrans since 1999, and to your knowledge,
this position has not been included in the designated positions for District +. iou recently
received notice that you are being required to file a Form 700. In years past, you have
voluntarily filed the Form 700, but this year you prefer to decline unless you are required to do
so by law.
You seek advice as to whether you are required to file a Form 700 if your position is not
designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. You also wish to know wheiher newly
enacted Regulation 18734 (Designated Employees and Consultants - positions pending Code
Amendment) applies to your position.
ANALYSIS
C onfli c t - of- I nt e r e st C o de s And F ilin g O b ligation s
One of the pu{poses of the Act is to ensure that public officials disclose their economic
interests. (Section 81002(c).) Public officials who make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions are required to file statements of economii interests whereby they
disclose information regarding their economic interests. (See Sections BTzOOet seq. and g7300
et seq.)
The first category of filers, govemed by Sections 87200 et seq., includes most high-
ranking elected officeholders. These 87200 filers (sometimes referred to as "statutory filers,,)
include, for example, elected state officers, judges, members of certain state commissions, heads
of local governments, those who manage public investments, and candidates for any of the
elected offices in this category. These officials are subject to the most expansive disclosure
requirements possible under the Act due to the nature of their duties.
The second category of filers, govemed by Sections 87300 et seq., covers all other
positions in an agency "which involve the making or participation in the making of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any iinancial interest. . .." (Seitio n g7302(a).)
People holding these positions are informally referred to as "designated.*ploy""r', and their
positions must be listed in conflict-of-interest codes, which most governmental agencies are
reqtrired to adopt and promulgate. (Section 87300; see Section siotg [defining..designated


File No. A-10-056
Page No. 3
employee"J.) Unlike most high-ranking officials, many designated employees are required to
make only limited disclosures of their economic interests, depending upon ttre duties associated
with their positions. These positions, and their respective aisctosure'requirements, are listed in
each agency's conflict of interest code.
Government Code Section 87300 requires every agency to adopt and promulgate a
conflict-of-interest code.2 Conflicrof-interest codes aie rlquiiea to identify tesignated
employees, and assign to those designated positions opp.opiiote categories of financial
disclosure. Each conflict-of-interest code must specifically enumeraie the positions within the
agency that involve making or participating in making gor"rn-.rtal decisions3 that *;r-- -
foreseeably have a material financial effect on any ecoriomic interest, and further set forth the
types of investments, business positions, income, and real property that are reportable by those
designated employees. (Section 57302.)
Your facts indicate that your position, which is classified as STS, is a classification that
has been in use since 1999 by your agency. You also state that your position is not a designated
classification in District 4, where you are employed.
Because the STS classification is not designated, there is no requirement that you file the
Form 700 until your position is added to your agency's conflict-of-inteiest code.
You did not include a job description or statement disclosing relevant job duties, nor did
you include information on whether your position is "decision-making" and sirould have been
included in your agency's conflict-of-interest code. Please note that we do not address this issue
in this request for advice. However, as your agency's code reviewing body, the FppC may
require your agency to review its conflict-of-intereit code for future urr"ni-".rts, and to add
positions not included.
2 The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act apply only to "public officials,,' including ..designated
employees." (Section 87100.) A "public official" is definid uy tni act to include .tvery *.ru"r, officer, employee
or consultant of a state or local government agency." (Section 82048.) The term "designated employee,' means any
officer, employee, member,_ or consultant of any agency who makes or participates in itre making of agency
decisions that may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest. (Section g2019.)
I A public otficial makes a governmental decision whenever the public official votes on a matter, appoints a
person' obligates or commits the agency to a course of action, or enters inio any 
"ortru.trui o!r."r"n, on behalf of
the agency' (Regulation l87o2.l(a).) Additionally, a public otficial participates in u gor..nrZniat decision when,
acting within the authority of the official's position, the public orficial negotiates, adiises, or makes
recommendations to the decisionmaker without significant substantive r.ui.* with respect to the actions enumerated
in Regulation 18700(b). This includes conducting research, making any investigation, or preparing or presenting any
report, analysis. or opinion that requires the exercise ofjudgment o=n the part oflhe official or designated employee
and the purpose of which is to influence the decision. lRegutation 1gi02.2.)


File No. A-10-056
Page No.4
Regulation 18734
Regulation 18734, which became effective on January l, z)l},requires agencies that
receive or create new positions to provide interim disclosure ior employ"., i, those positions.
The new positions are subject to the broadest disclosure category irrthe code, unless an agency,s
executive officer authorizes more narrow disclosure for new positions with limited duties.
Your facts indicate that the STS classification has existed in your agency since 1999.
Therefore, it is not a "new" position and Regulation lg734does not upply."
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (gt6) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Jrrnfrd lfr*
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
By:
ERjgl
cc:
Patti Oshita
Califomia Department of Transportation
